title: Caddie culture in India: your most important relationship
microFact: How you treat your caddie is the single most-watched social signal at an Indian golf club.
lesson:
At most Indian courses — Clover Greens included — you'll take a CADDIE. He carries your bag, finds your ball, rakes bunkers, fills divots, reads greens, and knows the course better than most members.

The protocol:

• Ask his name on the first tee and use it all round.
• Caddie FEE is paid at booking or the pro shop; the TIP is separate and yours to give directly — ₹300–500 for 18 holes is standard, more for great green-reading or a hot day.
• Listen to his reads: "one ball left" means aim one ball-width left of the hole. On his home course, trust him over your own eyes.
• Never blame him for your shots. Members are silently watching exactly this.
• At the end: thank him by name, tip in hand, not tossed.

A caddie who likes you will save you five strokes and teach you the course. One who doesn't will carry your bag in silence. Entirely your choice.

quiz.question: Your caddie says the putt is 'two balls right'. What does he mean?
options: Aim two ball-widths right of the hole
Hit it twice as hard as it looks
The green slopes right two degrees
Use two balls to practice the line
quiz.explanation: Green reads are given in ball-widths or cup-widths off the edge of the hole. Aim there; the slope brings it back.
